text: Build boring systems that solve interesting problems.
Everything is an experiment.
Complexity compounds. Simplicity scales.
AI should remove friction, not create it.
Good tooling feels invisible.
Unknown places are often the most interesting ones.
Ship it. Then make it great.
The best architecture is the one you can rewrite.
Automate the boring parts. Focus on the interesting ones.
Infrastructure should be boring until it isn't.
Write code that reads like a good conversation.
The goal is not to use AI. The goal is to solve problems.
Every system is a distributed system. Some just don't know it yet.
Developer experience is user experience for builders.
The best abstraction is the one you don't need.
Start with the problem. The solution will follow.
Code is communication. Optimize for clarity.
Build tools that make other developers faster.
The future of development is local-first.
Small services. Big outcomes.
Unknown places are where the best discoveries happen.
The best API is the one that disappears.
Simplicity is the ultimate sophistication.
Build for the long term. Ship for today.
AI is a tool, not a replacement for thinking.
The best systems are the ones you forget are there.
Every line of code is a liability. Make it count.
Open source is a conversation with the world.
Unknown places are where the best code lives.
The best tools teach you how to use them.
